skill.name: super-swarm-spark
skill.description: Orchestrates parallel task execution using up to 12 concurrent Sparky subagents. Parses development plan files and delegates tasks in a rolling pool for rapid project completion.
skill.summary: Parallel task orchestrator for running up to 12 concurrent AI subagents on development plans
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: All 22 static findings are false positives. The scanner misinterpreted markdown backticks as shell execution commands and generated spurious crypto algorithm warnings. This skill is a legitimate agent orchestrator that manages parallel subagent execution for development tasks. No malicious behavior detected.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: Static scanner incorrectly flagged lines as containing weak cryptographic algorithms. Analysis shows NO cryptographic code exists in this skill. Lines flagged (3, 38, 44, 66, 93, 126) contain only orchestration instructions and task prompts.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual code review confirms no cryptographic algorithms present. Scanner likely matched on unrelated keywords in markdown.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands (Markdown Backticks)
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' but these are markdown code formatting (backticks around words like `pending`, `running`). No actual shell execution occurs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks are markdown inline code syntax, not shell command execution. This is a known scanner limitation with markdown files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged line 71 mentioning 'launch' as system reconnaissance. This is standard agent orchestration behavior, not reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill launches Sparky subagents for task execution, which is the intended orchestration behavior, not malicious reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Run 12 Parallel AI Agents on Your Dev Plan
content.value_statement: Executing large development plans sequentially is slow. This skill orchestrates up to 12 concurrent AI subagents that work in parallel on different tasks, dramatically speeding up project completion while maintaining coordination.
actual_capabilities: Parses markdown plan files and extracts task definitions with IDs, descriptions, and acceptance criteria
Manages a rolling pool of up to 12 concurrent Sparky subagents
Builds context packs with canonical file paths to prevent naming conflicts across parallel tasks
Validates each subagent completion and updates the plan file with progress
Performs final integration pass to reconcile conflicts from parallel execution
limitations: Only works with Sparky subagent role (not other agent types)
Requires plan files in specific markdown format with task subsections
Does not handle complex dependency graphs between tasks
Cannot execute without a valid plan file

use_cases.title: Accelerate Large Feature Implementations
use_cases.description: When implementing a large feature that can be broken into independent tasks, use this skill to run multiple subagents in parallel, reducing total execution time significantly.
use_cases.target_user: Developers working on substantial features with multiple independent components

use_cases.title: Parallelize Test Writing
use_cases.description: Run multiple subagents simultaneously to write tests for different modules or components of a codebase in parallel.
use_cases.target_user: QA engineers and developers needing comprehensive test coverage quickly

use_cases.title: Batch Refactoring Tasks
use_cases.description: Execute multiple refactoring tasks across different parts of the codebase concurrently while maintaining naming consistency through context packs.
use_cases.target_user: Software architects performing large-scale code improvements

prompt_templates.title: Run Full Development Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute the plan file ./my-project-plan.md using super-swarm-spark. Run all tasks in the plan.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have a complete development plan and want to execute all tasks in parallel

prompt_templates.title: Run Specific Tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute tasks T1, T3, and T5 from ./backend-plan.md using super-swarm-spark.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you only want to run a subset of tasks from a larger plan

prompt_templates.title: Continue After Partial Completion
prompt_templates.prompt: Continue executing ./feature-plan.md. The following tasks are already complete: T1, T2. Run the remaining pending tasks.
prompt_templates.scenario: When some tasks finished and you want to continue with remaining work

prompt_templates.title: Integration and Testing Pass
prompt_templates.prompt: After all subagents complete their tasks in ./full-plan.md, perform the final integration pass and run tests to ensure everything works together.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want the orchestrator to handle integration after parallel task execution

output_examples.output:
Starting parallel execution with 12 Sparky agents...

Launched: T1 (User Model), T2 (Auth Controller), T3 (JWT Handler)...

Task T1 completed: Created User model with validation
Task T2 completed: Implemented auth endpoints

Integration pass: Resolved 2 path conflicts

Final status: 8/8 tasks completed, all tests passing

Running subset: T1, T2, T4

Launched 3 concurrent agents...

All tasks completed successfully.
Updated plan file with completion logs.

best_practices: Ensure plan files have clear task IDs (T1, T2, etc.) and complete acceptance criteria for each task
Provide canonical file paths in the plan to help the orchestrator prevent naming conflicts
Keep tasks relatively independent to maximize parallel execution benefits
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for plans with heavy dependencies between tasks (subagents will block waiting on each other)
Avoid running this skill without a properly formatted plan file
Do not expect the orchestrator to handle tasks requiring the same file simultaneously

faq.question: What is the difference between this skill and other orchestration skills?
faq.answer: This skill uses a rolling pool of up to 12 concurrent Sparky subagents with explicit path coordination to prevent filename drift across parallel tasks.

faq.question: Can I use this with agent types other than Sparky?
faq.answer: No. This skill explicitly requires the sparky agent role and will not work with other agent types.

faq.question: How does the skill handle file name conflicts?
faq.answer: The orchestrator builds context packs with canonical file paths and enforces exact naming in all subagent prompts to prevent conflicts.

faq.question: What happens if a subagent task fails?
faq.answer: The orchestrator validates each completion, detects failures, and can retry or escalate failed tasks before proceeding.

faq.question: Do I need to push my commits after the skill runs?
faq.answer: No. The skill instructs subagents to commit but never push. You review and push changes after verification.

faq.question: Can I run a subset of tasks from my plan?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide task IDs after the plan file (e.g., /super-swarm-spark plan.md T1 T3 T5) to run only those tasks.
